name: Rep. Andy Levin

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: Is it a perfect document? No. For starters, I think we need to do much more to tackle our bloated military budget, so we can invest more in our schools, our roads & good-paying jobs. But it's a big step in the right direction. Let's go!

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: This is a moment unlike any in our lifetimes. We need to rebuild our outdated infrastructure, our middle class & our economy, so it works for everyone. The president's budget shows he's ready to meet that moment!

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: Environmental/Sustainable/Governance (ESG) investing is simple: stop investing without consideration of wider effects and invest in companies that do good in the world. My #SustainableInvestment bills promotes transparency in how funds invest using ESG.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: The struggle to stop fossil fuel companies from burning the planet is an international project, and this is great news from the Netherlands. Oil and gas companies have made clear by their actions they will not take climate change seriously until forced by the people and the law.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: The GOP’s infrastructure counteroffer is a painful reminder that one of the two political parties in the most powerful country in the world remains fully committed to overheating the planet, endangering billions of people and causing climate catastrophe.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: Yesterday, I spoke with @StateDept, the Burmese ambassador to the U.S.  and our ambassador to Burma. Danny Fenster’s immediate and unconditional release is a top priority. Thank you for your calls and emails. Please know I will not stop fighting until Danny is home.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: To become the global leader in clean energy the U.S. must invest in clean energy R&D. @SenWarren and I are introducing the NICE Act to create the National Institutes of Clean Energy, which will develop the advanced green tech of the future. #NICEAct #GreenFutureNow

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: It’s time to convert our nation’s biggest spender, the U.S. government, to American-made clean energy products.  @SenWarren’s and my #BuyGreenAct will kick-start demand for renewables by committing our govt to purchase $1.5 trillion in green goods. #GreenFutureNow

name: Rep. Andy Levin

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: Today marks the anniversary of 100,000 Americans perishing from COVID. For a long time after, the pandemic only got worse. But the American people persevered. Now, with vaccination rates on the rise and local economies reopening, we have a chance to put this pandemic behind us.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: George Floyd died a year ago, sparking the largest protest movement in American history. There is nothing but pain and frustration when I think of George’s death. But when I think about how this nation responded—united in its rejection of his murder—I’m hopeful for the future.

name: Rep. Andy Levin

tweet: It’s amazing that Congress has the opportunity to empower tens of millions of Americans to join a union, create better workplaces and raise wages all with one piece of legislation. It’s time to pass the PRO Act. #PassthePROAct

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: Did I miss anything @Amazon?

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: More (remember, Amazon calls these workers “independent”):  ❌Restrictions for terminating the business relationship during the busy season ❌Mandatory at-will employment so workers can be fired any time for no reason ❌Limited press statements unless Amazon gives permission.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: Surely this must be the end of the list (it’s not):  ❌Mandatory non-disclosure agreements ❌In-vehicle surveillance cameras ❌Cancellation fees ❌Mandatory arbitration for contractors and their workers

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: And more after that:  ❌Access to and unlimited use of data including location, speed, and GPS tracking, even after the business relationship ends ❌Indemnification of Amazon in serious cases where someone is hurt or dies ❌Control over litigation strategy if a lawsuit arises

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: And a few more:  ❌Workers’ social media posts   ❌Use Amazon-approved training ❌Use of Amazon’s delivery app ❌Mandatory drug tests for workers whenever Amazon wants ❌Physical access to their premises

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: A not-so-brief list of ways Amazon controls supposedly “independent” contractors and their workers, per @josheidelson:  ❌Workers’ personal grooming ❌Workers’ usage of perfume/cologne ❌Workers’ dental hygiene ❌Workers’ fingernails and hair

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: This is exactly what we mean when we talk about worker misclassification. Drivers are “independent” except that Amazon controls basically every aspect of how they operate and can unilaterally change any of these working conditions on a whim. All while avoiding federal law.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: “It is hereby declared to be the policy of the United States to…encourag[e] the practice and procedure of collective bargaining and by protecting the exercise by workers of full freedom of association, self-organization, and designation of representatives of their own choosing”

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: As a result, economic inequality has soared. Strong unions helped reduce poverty & build the middle class. The subsequent degradation of union rights has helped shrink it. The #PROAct would do so much to reverse these trends—including the scourge of racial & gender inequality.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: The fact is that way too many business interests are afraid of workers’ collective power to demand higher wages, better benefits & safer working conditions. That’s why they engage in coercive, often illegal, union busting campaigns & spend $340M/year on union busting consultants.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: The sad reality is that over the past 86 years, not one provision; not one word; not one comma has been added to the law to advance private sector workers’ freedom to form unions & bargain collectively. Instead we’ve had 86 long years of the systematic erosion of workers’ rights.

tweet: I started walking north, until I got to a point where the subway was operating or I could get a cab or just kept walking - I honestly don’t remember how I made it home to Silver Spring.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: Out in the sea of people on the street, after a period of group commiseration with colleagues and strangers alike, I decided I needed to get to my family ASAP.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: I remember discussing whether we should walk to the Pentagon to help — it would probably have taken an hour just to get there on foot — with Damon Silvers, who works at the AFL-CIO to this day as policy director.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: I Street and 16th Street were filled with people streaming out of buildings. The subway had been shut down. Most of us didn’t have cars, and anyway the streets were clogged.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: At some point the building was evacuated. People realized that the White House was a target and we were in danger. I remember gathering my things as my colleagues who were emergency building captains urged us to hasten outside, but once we were there, there was nowhere to go.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: We were all wandering around the 8th Floor in a daze, not knowing what to do. A while later, we went back into the conference room with its huge south-facing windows and looked sharply to the right, or west, where you could see a huge plume of smoke — the Pentagon on fire.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: I remember going down the hall to Arlene Holt-Baker’s office with her, because she had a TV. We started watching what was happening, and saw the completely incredible sight on live television of the second commercial jet hitting the south tower.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: The image in my head and I bet the heads of others as well was of a biplane or other little tourist plane, because there had been a recent incident like that. Maybe then or maybe a few minutes later, Bob said, “I guess we should finish up and adjourn the meeting.”

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: But Bob sort of cocked his head and said to the group but also to no one in particular, because it was so odd, “Hmmm, a plane hit the World Trade Center.”

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: President Sweeney’s chief of staff, Bob Welsh, was presiding, as usual. I remember Bob’s secretary, as she would still have been called then, Linda Hall, entering the room and passing Bob a note. That wasn’t uncommon.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: I was the Assistant Director of Organizing at the AFL-CIO. By 9/11, I had already worked there for six years, since John Sweeney had become president of the labor federation in October, 1995. So it was as regular a part of my life as almost anything, those management meetings.

name: Rep. Andy Levin
tweet: September 11, 2001 was a Tuesday. I remember because that’s the day the senior staff of the @AFLCIO met in the executive conference room on the 8th floor at 815 16th Street. The room has one of the great views in Washington, overlooking the White House across Lafayette Square.
